description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rugged, porous surface and a mix of gray and brown hues. The stone features a prominent bulge and several crevices, with some areas appearing more eroded and pitted than others. Its texture is coarse, with visible granular inclusions and a few small cavities, suggesting volcanic or weathered sedimentary origin. The coloration is mottled, with darker gray patches interspersed with lighter, earthier tones, and some subtle layering is visible in certain areas. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one side more rounded and the other more jagged, giving it a natural, unworked appearance. This stone would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features, serving as a natural obstacle or decorative element.
description: A rugged, irregularly shaped stone with a coarse, pitted surface and mottled gray-brown coloration. Its asymmetrical form and weathered texture suggest natural origin and outdoor placement.
description_view_0: This view highlights a prominent bulge and a deep crevice, with visible layering and a mix of gray and brown tones.
description_view_1: The stone appears more jagged and porous from this angle, with rougher texture and less visible layering.
description_view_2: A rounded, smoother side is visible here, with fewer deep crevices and a more uniform surface coloration.
description_view_3: This side shows a flatter, broader surface with subtle mottling and less pronounced surface features.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on soil or grass
attribute_pattern: Mottled coloration with subtle layering and granular inclusions; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible erosion, indicating prolonged exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, grass, or other stones, often partially embedded or surrounded by vegetation.
